input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest .NET SDK versions
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (e.g., .NET 8 or 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls Android SDK, build tools, emulator)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for WinUI/Windows App SDK compilation
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK `
              --includeRecommended --quiet --wait"
```

## 4. Configure Android SDK (if not using VS-managed SDK)

```powershell
# The MAUI workload installs Android SDK here by default:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"

# Discover installed platforms/build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific API level dynamically (latest stable)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools"

# Accept licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", 
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\jdk\microsoft_corp\17*", "User")

# Refresh current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
```

## 6. Enable Developer Mode (for Windows deployment)

```powershell
# Requires admin
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 7. Create and Verify a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n TestMaui
cd TestMaui

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 8. (Optional) Android Emulator Setup

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | 
  Select-String "system-images"

# Install an image
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch emulator
Start-Process "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_MAUI"
```

---

**Key version-discovery tips:**
- `dotnet workload search` — shows workload names/versions
- `sdkmanager --list` — shows all available Android components
- `dotnet --list-sdks` — confirms installed SDK versions
- `winget upgrade` — shows if any tools have updates

Run steps 1–6 in an **elevated** PowerShell, then step 7+ as your normal user.
